mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate at 0.08% represents an emergency-level monetary accommodation. As long as FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days through mechanical valuation expansion.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of periods where FEDFUNDS < 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any rolling 12-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate at 3.8% signals a tight labor market near full employment. When UNRATE remains below 4.0%, wage pressures and consumer confidence sustain demand, producing positive equity returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations remain elevated.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean return < 1.5%) when UNRATE < 4.0%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts at 1,638K units signal robust residential investment demand. When HOUST exceeds 1,500K annualized, construction employment and consumer wealth effects drive positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through multiplier effects on aggregate demand.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following HOUST > 1500 threshold crossings are not significantly positive (mean < 0.8%), or signal triggers in > 40% of months without producing positive returns.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production at 103.47 (2017=100) reflects strong manufacturing capacity utilization. When INDPRO declines more than 1.5% over 3 months, it signals demand weakness ahead, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as forward earnings expectations compress.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where INDPRO declines > 1.5% over 3 months, or Rank IC > -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales at 649,776M and payroll employment at 150,399K show strong consumer spending capacity. When both RSAFS and PAYEMS grow simultaneously above their 3-month trend, the joint signal indicates robust aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through earnings growth acceleration.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both > 3-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 649776M and PAYEMS at 150399K as of 2007-07-15
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns when both RSAFS and PAYEMS exceed 3-month MA are not significantly positive (mean < 1.0%), or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in regression analysis.

mechanism_narrative: CPI at 284.18 (1982-1984=100) reflects cumulative inflation. When CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.4% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS remains below 1.0%, the Fed is behind the inflation curve, creating policy uncertainty that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as real discount rates compress and volatility rises.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 284.18 and FEDFUNDS at 0.08% as of 2007-08-15
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where both conditions are met, or the interaction coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment at 150,399K with unemployment at 3.8% indicates a labor market at or near capacity. When PAYEMS growth decelerates below 0.1% month-over-month for two consecutive months, it signals demand weakness ahead, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations reset lower.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS MoM growth < 0.1% for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following the trigger are not significantly negative (mean > -0.5%), or signal fails to trigger in any 12-month period despite meeting threshold criteria.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP at 19,810.6B (chained 2017 dollars) with housing starts at 1,638K and retail sales at 649,776M show broad-based demand strength. When HOUST and RSAFS both decline more than 2% from their 3-month average simultaneously, it signals demand shock that produces negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as forward earnings estimates compress sharply.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both decline > 2% from 3-month MA
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1638K and RSAFS at 649776M as of 2007-07-15
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances where both HOUST and RSAFS decline > 2% from 3-month MA, or interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.12).
